title: ds-viz-singlevalueicon - option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.singlevalueicon. Each panel exercises one option group: icon (default vs. uploaded kvstore SVG), iconColor + iconOpacity + iconPosition (before / after), majorValue + unit + underLabel, trendDisplay (absolute / percent / off), shouldAbbreviateTrendValue + thousand separators, showValue (icon-only mode), majorFontSize, dynamic colour via DOS / rangeValue. SPL is makeresults-based. Every non-default panel uses the SAME uploaded check icon - the test bench shows option behaviour, not glyph variety, because real kvstore icon URLs are per-instance and only obtainable through the Splunk UI's icon picker.

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval revenue = round(8000 + 2000*sin(i/4) + random()%500) | table _time revenue

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval conversion_rate = round(2.5 + sin(i/4)*0.4 + random()%10/100, 2) | table _time conversion_rate

ds_currency_kpi.name: currency kpi - large value with trend

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval mrr = round(1245000 + i*1500 + random()%2000) | table _time mrr
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval health = round(65 + 28*sin(i/4) + random()%5) | table _time health
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval errors = round(12 + sin(i/3)*8 + random()%5) | table _time errors

options.markdown:
# `splunk.singlevalueicon` - option matrix

A `splunk.singlevalue` with a leading icon. The 10.4 reference exposes 16 options - the singlevalue option set MINUS `sparkline*` (no sparkline) and `backgroundColor` defaults to **transparent** (vs. theme background for singlevalue), PLUS four icon options: `icon`, `iconColor`, `iconOpacity`, `iconPosition`. **Only renders inside Absolute layout.**

## icon: only two reliable forms on this build (Splunk Enterprise 10.2.1)

1. `"default"` - generic Splunk square. Works everywhere, no upload needed.
2. `"splunk-enterprise-kvstore://<icon-name>__<UUID>.svg"` - URL pointing at an SVG already uploaded to the `splunk_dashboard_icons` KV-store collection. The UUID is generated at upload time and cannot be invented; you obtain a real URL by uploading or picking an icon through the Dashboard Studio editor's icon picker.

Other forms tested and **not rendering** on this build: `data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg ...>` data URIs, bare 24-hex `splunk-enterprise-kvstore://abcd1234...` ObjectIds, bare named tokens (`thermometerFull`).

The panels below all use the same uploaded check icon (`icon-check__e29f784a-31a2-4544-813f-efce24d5be32.svg`) so the option behaviour stays the focus. Replace the `icon` URL with your own picker output to vary the glyph.

viz_default_icon.title: 1. icon - default + showValue (default)
viz_default_icon.description: icon = 'default' renders the generic Splunk square. showValue = true (default) renders the major value + trend below the icon. This is the only icon form that needs no per-instance upload.

viz_kvstore_check.title: 2. kvstore filename URL - uploaded SVG
viz_kvstore_check.description: icon = splunk-enterprise-kvstore://<name>__<UUID>.svg. This is the only non-default form that renders on this build. The UUID is generated when you upload the SVG via the Dashboard Studio editor's icon picker. iconColor still applies because Splunk-uploaded icons carry currentColor strokes.

viz_icon_position_after.title: 3. iconPosition - after
viz_icon_position_after.description: iconPosition = 'after' moves the icon to the right of the value. Default is 'before'. Use 'after' when the icon is a meaningful suffix - a units glyph, a state indicator. Same uploaded check icon, just on the right side.

viz_icon_only.title: 4. showValue - false (icon-only)
viz_icon_only.description: showValue = false hides BOTH the major value AND the trend - the panel is just the icon. Useful for status-light panels or visual breakers in a KPI strip. Note the uploaded check icon still renders large; the underlying glyph design controls the icon size, not the panel.

viz_dynamic_iconColor.title: 5. Dynamic iconColor via rangeValue
viz_dynamic_iconColor.description: iconColor uses Dynamic Options Syntax to map the current value to a colour: green >= 80, amber 60-80, red < 60. Pattern: '> primary | seriesByName(field) | lastPoint() | rangeValue(thresholds)'. Disjoint, gap-free buckets: 'to' is exclusive (< X), 'from' is inclusive (>= X). The kvstore icon retains currentColor on its stroke so iconColor takes effect.

viz_dynamic_majorColor.title: 6. Dynamic majorColor (number colour, fixed icon)
viz_dynamic_majorColor.description: iconColor stays a calm hue, majorColor flips on threshold: green < 10, amber 10-20, red >= 20 errors. Note thresholds run inverse here - lower is better, so green is the 'to' bucket. Reads as 'something is wrong with this metric' without losing the icon's identity. Common SOC pattern.

viz_iconOpacity.title: 7. iconOpacity 0.3 - muted secondary metric
viz_iconOpacity.description: iconOpacity = 0.3 fades the icon. Use when the icon is decorative / categorisation rather than the message. The eye lands on the value first, the icon is context.

viz_no_trend.title: 8. trendDisplay - off (static KPI with icon)
viz_no_trend.description: Single-row data source, no time dimension. trendDisplay = 'off' suppresses the delta. The icon supplies meaning without needing recent history.

viz_majorFontSize.title: 9. majorFontSize - 56 (compact KPI strip)
viz_majorFontSize.description: majorFontSize = 56 locks the value font size. Icon stays auto-sized. Useful when a row of singlevalueicon tiles needs visual consistency regardless of underlying value magnitude.

viz_dynamic_bg.title: 10. Dynamic backgroundColor + locked majorColor
viz_dynamic_bg.description: Whole-tile background flips on threshold: dark < 10, amber 10-20, red >= 20 errors. majorColor + iconColor lock to white so the tile stays readable when the bg goes dark. Use sparingly - one or two of these is enough per dashboard.

viz_separators_off.title: 11. shouldUseThousandSeparators - false
viz_separators_off.description: shouldUseThousandSeparators = false strips commas. Use for IDs, sequence numbers, version numbers. Numbers like build IDs (1245000) read as IDs, not quantities, when commas are absent.

viz_kpi_strip_compact.title: 12. Compact KPI strip pattern
viz_kpi_strip_compact.description: Transparent background + small majorFontSize + icon-after layout. Used for rows of small KPI tiles in a dense dashboard header strip. Combines several options at once.
